questions.question_text: Find the value of m which satisfies $$\left(\frac{26}{7}\right)^5 \times \left(\frac{7}{26}\right)^{11} \times \left(\frac{26}{7}\right)^{19} = \left(\frac{7}{26}\right)^{9m+17}$$
If the simple interest on a sum of ₹9,800 is ₹16,562 for a period of 13 years, find the rate of interest per annum.
If $$\sec\theta+\cos\theta=a$$ and $$\tan\theta+\cot\theta=b$$, find the value of $$\cosec2\theta$$.
A parallelogram has sides 12 cm and 9 cm long. The length of one of its diagonals is 15 cm What is the area (in $$cm^2$$) of the parallelogram?
Find the positive integral value of x if the fourth proportional of x, x + 1, 2x + 3 is y and y = 3x + 3.
A jacket has a marked price of ₹5,000. The shopkeeper offers two successive discounts of 20% and 30%. What is the final selling price?
Find the value of $$\frac{1.4 \times 1.4 \times 1.4 - 1.2 \times 1.2 \times 1.2}{1.4 \times 1.4 + 1.4 \times 1.2 + 1.2 \times 1.2}$$
The mean proportional of 0.15 and 0.000135 is ____.
Priyal is 5 years younger than her brother. After 4 years, the sum of their ages will be 39 years. What is Priyal's current age?
Rohit walks at a speed of 6 km/hr. How much time will he take to cover 12 km?
An article is marked for ₹2,000. A 25% discount is offered on the marked price, followed by a second discount of 10% on the new price. What is the final selling price of the article?

Sides PQ and QR of $$\triangle PQR$$ are produced to points S and T. If $$\angle PRT > \angle RQS > 50^\circ$$ and $$\angle RPQ = 100^\circ - 2 \angle RQS$$, which of the following statements is true?

The frequency distribution of the hourly wages rate of 100 employees of a paper mill is follows. Find the median wage rate (in ₹). Wages rates(₹) 54-56 56-58 58-60 60-62 62-64 Number of workers 20 20 20 20 20

Two taps can fill a cistern in 4 hours and 15 hours, respectively. A third tap can empty it in 12 hours. How long (in hours) will it take to fill one-fourth of the empty cistern if all the taps are opened together?
A stationery shop sells notebooks and pens. The cost of 4 notebooks and 6 pens is ₹228. The cost of 7 notebooks and 8 pens is ₹333. Find the cost of one notebook.

A string initially in the shape of a circle with a radius of 21 cm is cut into two distinct pieces. The first piece is converted into a rectangle whose length is 18 cm and breadth is 14 cm, while the second piece is formed into a square. If the perimeter of the rectangle is 24 cm more than the perimeter of the square, find the breadth of the rectangle. (Use $$\pi = \frac{22}{7}$$)
What is the mean proportional between 0.16 and 0.49?
A trader sells goods at a 15% profit but uses a weight of 950 grams for a kilogram. What is his total profit percentage? (Correct to two decimal places.)

The cost of an article is increased by 20% in the first year. In the second year the increased cost is decreased by 24%. If the final cost of the article is ₹1,938, find the original cost of the article.
The HCF and LCM of two numbers are 27 and 324, respectively. If one number is 81, find the other number.

Rajiv invested a certain sum of money at 10% per annum. After 2 years, he received ₹190.5 as compound interest. Compound annually. Find the corresponding simple interest (in ₹) for 2 years at the same rate.
A teacher records the number of books read by each student in a month: 3, 5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 3, 2, 4, 5. What is the mode of this data set?
If $$\frac{x}{x+1} + \frac{x+1}{x} = \frac{5}{2}$$, then find x.
Harish bought an article for ₹347 and sold it to Renu at a loss of 6%. With this amount, Harish bought another article and sold it at a gain of 65%. What is Harish's overall gain percentage?

A train met with an accident 50 km away from station A. It completed the remaining journey at three-fourths of the original speed and reached station 35 minutes late. Had the accident occurred 24 km further down the line, it would have been only 25 minutes late. What is the original speed of the train?

The price of fuel decreases by 45%, 50% and 25% in three successive months, but increases by 20% in the fourth month. What is the percentage decrease in the price of fuel in the fourth month as compared to its original price? (Round off to 2 decimal places.)
Pipe X can fill a reservoir in 15 hours and pipe Y can empty it in 20 hours. If both pipes are opened simultaneously and pipe Y is closed after 10 hours, find the total time to fill the reservoir.
If the ratio of two numbers is 4 : 1, and their HCF is 47, then their LCM is:
Which of the following is a large scale industry?
Which of the following temple complexes is known for its 24-metre-high vimana and stone image of Shiva?
In 2025, which stratovolcano in New Zealand was granted individual legal status, becoming the third natural entity in the country to receive personhood?
According to Census 2011, which Union Territory had the lowest literacy rate?
In which of the following years did the Tata Group take over state-owned Air India?
In which state were the $$77^{th}$$ Senior and $$54^{th}$$ Junior National Track Cycling Championships held from 19 to 24 December 2025?
State Public Service Commissions' members are appointed by the governor and removed by whom?
India's industrial growth remained sluggish from the mid-1960s to 1980. What term is often used to describe this slow pace of economic growth?
If an object having a mass of 10 kg is moving with a velocity of 50 m/s, find the correct momentum of the object.
Ustad Amjad Ali Khan is world-renowned for playing which of the following instruments?
In which region of Gujarat are Duha, Sorathaa, Chaand, Belaads, etc. traditionally performed in folk music?
Who set a new record for the most sixes in the ICC Men's ODI events in March 2025?
The Munda Uprising (1899–1900) was associated with which leader?
Which of the following are the official languages of India?

options.text: Application software is required to start the computer, whereas system software is used only after the computer has fully booted up.
System software is always free and open-source, but application software must always be purchased with a license.
System software manages the computer's core resources and provides a platform for other programs, while application software is designed to perform specific tasks for the user.
System software is developed by large companies, while application software is developed only by individual programmers.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements correctly highlights the primary difference between System Software and Application Software?
Which continent is known as the 'Island Continent'?

options.text: It was removed from Fundamental Rights
It was elevated to a Directive Principle
It was given more enforcement power

questions.question_text: What major change was made to the Right to Property by the 44th Amendment Act (1978)?
When a metal spoon is placed in a hot cup of tea, it soon becomes warm. Which mode of heat transfer is primarily responsible for this?
Which of the following desert features forms due to parallel retreat of slopes through backwasting?

options.text: To improve interoperability against maritime threats

questions.question_text: What was the primary objective of Exercise INDRA, 2025?

The President may return a non-money bill to Parliament for reconsideration for how many times?
The Capability Approach, which defines development as expanding individuals' freedoms, was first proposed by which economist?

options.text: National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences, Bengaluru
Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research, Chandigarh
All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Delhi

questions.question_text: Which organisation led the bladder cancer radiation therapy trial in November 2025?
The work Tarikh-i-Rashidi, which provides valuable information about Central Asia and early Mughal history, was written by:
World Water Day, observed on 22 March 2025, highlighted concerns related to:
Which of the following organizations launched the 'Frontier Seed' initiative to boost private sector growth and ocean impact in the Pacific in 2025?
Which of the following provisions of the Constitution of India provides the President with the power to grant pardons, reprieves or remissions?

options.text: Emergence of polished stone axes associated with settled agricultural villages
Use of microlithic tools along with bow-and-arrow and plant-processing implements
Manufacture of heavy flake tools similar to Lower Paleolithic hand-axes
Adoption of iron tools indicates an early transition to metal-using cultures

questions.question_text: Which innovation correctly illustrates Mesolithic advances in subsistence and technology in India?

Which Indian state became the first in the country to enact its own Right to Information Act in 1997?

Under the Legal Services Authorities Act of which year, every State / District Authority or the Supreme Court / High Court Legal Services Committee or Taluk Legal Services Committee may organise Lok Adalats, as the case may be?
What do we call the lasting of sound caused by repeated reflections?
Which shortcut is used to quickly search for a word in a webpage?

In physical science, understanding the dimension of a quantity helps to analyze formulas and relationships between variables. Which of the dimensions correctly represents the physical quantity 'Force' in terms of mass, length, and time?
Who is the author of the book titled 'Modi's Mission'?
Which type of file is identified by the extension .exe in Windows 11?

options.text: Establishment of provincial autonomy, complete financial authority, and autonomous ministries
Expansion of legislative councils, admission of Indians to Viceroy's executive council, and new deliberative powers.
Reduction of legislative council size, replacement of budgetary rights, and elimination of Indian representatives.
Abolition of nominated seats, imposition of direct elections, and removal of official majorities.

questions.question_text: Which outcome resulted from the Indian Councils Act of 1909 (Morley–Minto Reforms)?
Which of the following is an example of computers in the service industry?
In certain code language, 'CHIN' is coded as '9413' and 'NICE' is coded as '2319'. What is the code for 'IE' in that language?
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?ZEK UZF PUA KPV ?
If 'A' stands for '+', 'B' stands for 'x', 'C' stands for '+' and 'D' stands for '-', then the resultant of which of the following would be 4?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?(Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
In certain code language, 'uncle steak gun' is coded as 'xl wc iv' and 'sir gum barn' is coded as 'wc mn uf'. How is 'gum' coded in the given language?

A, B, D, J, O, T and Z are sitting around a circular table facing the center. Exactly three people sit between D and T when counted from the right of Z. J sits second to the left of T. A sits immediately to the left of J. D is not an immediate neighbor of T. How many people sit between B and A when counted from the left of A?
CZAZ is related to YBTS in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, EBCB is related to XUVI. To which of the following is CIDED related, following the same logic?

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets.(NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down and/substracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)(17, 4, 204)(19, 5, 285)

Six boxes H,I,J,K,L and E are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. E is kept immediately above J. I is kept immediately above L. Only K is kept above H. Only two boxes are kept above E. How many boxes are kept between I and K?

Seven boxes Q, H, I, J, K, L and T are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only two boxes are kept above L and only T is kept above I. No box is kept above J. H is kept at some place below G but at some place above K. How many boxes are kept between I and H?

Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).Statements:All ribs are carpets.No carpet is a jacket.Conclusions:(I) No hat is a locker.(II) Some lockers are carpets.

Refer to the given series and answer the question that follows. All numbers are single-digit numbers only.(Left) 8 5 2 8 8 2 3 1 8 8 9 6 4 8 6 2 9 9 2 4 6 9 (Right)How many such odd digits are there, each of which is immediately preceded by an odd digit and also immediately followed by an odd digit?

Refer to the following letter series and answer the question.(Left) T E V I W X S F Y P D G H K R Z O L M S (Right)How many letters are there in the English alphabetical series between the letter which is sixth from the right end and fourth from the left end of the series?

If 1 is added to each odd digit and 2 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 4317886, how many digits will appear more than once in the new number thus formed?

Each letter in the word INJURED is arranged in the English alphabetical order. How many letters are there in the English alphabetical series between the letter which is third from the left and the one which is second from the right in the new letter-cluster thus formed?

In a certain code language,A @ B means 'A is the mother of B',A & B means 'A is the sister of B',A + B means 'A is the brother of B' andA = B means 'A is the father of B'.How is S related to H if S + D + R @ Y x H'?

Shikshin starts from Point Y and drives 75 km towards East. He then takes a left turn, drives 36 km, turns and drives 44 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 43 km. He takes a right turn, drives 37 km. He then turns left, drives 26 km to stop at Point Z. How far (absolute distance) and in which direction should he drive in order to reach Point Y again? (All turns are 90° turns only unless specified.)

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?(Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster pairs.)

Eight people S, T, U, V, W, X, Y and Z are sitting around a circular table, facing the centre. S sits third to the left of W. T sits third to the right of V. X is an immediate neighbour of U and V. Z sits second to the left of S. How many people sit between X and W, when counted from the left of X?

Which is the one that does not belong to that group?(Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)

Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).Statements:All ribs are carpets.No carpet is a jacket.Conclusions:(I) No hat is a locker.(II) Some lockers are carpets.
